name: Artichoke & Cheese Stuffed Squash
recipeIngredient: 10 oz mushrooms, cleaned and finely chopped
1/8 teaspoon fresh ground black pepper, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Cut squashes in half horizontally and place each half cut-side down in baking pans at least two-inches deep which have been oiled lightly on the bottom. Add about one-half inch of boiling water to pan.
3. Bake at 375°F until very soft but not altogether mushy when tested with a sharp knife, about 45 minutes to one hour depending on size of squash.
4. Prepare filling while squashes bake: Drain artichokes, rinse, and chop coarsely. Heat 3 tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add mushrooms, artichoke hearts and celery and saute until vegetables are soft, about 5 minutes. Add scallions, garlic, basil, oregano, thyme, sage and soy sauce and continue to cook another 3 minutes. Add pepper and stir. Remove skillet from heat and stir in cheeses until melted and combined. Stir in breadcrumbs, adding up to 4 tablespoons water until well moistened and combined.
5. Remove squash from oven. Remove from pan and place cut-side up on cutting board. When cool enough to handle, cut a thin slice from the bottom of each squash half (if necessary) to allow them to sit flat.
6. Scoop out seeds with a spoon and discard.
7. Fill squash cavities with stuffing mixture, distributing evenly among four squash halves.
8. Place squash halves, stuffing-side up, in an oiled baking pan big enough to hold them. If not baking immediately, refrigerate until about 30 minutes before serving.
9. Bake in 375°F oven for 20-30 minutes, or until hot. If taking from refrigerator, this will take 30-40 minutes.
10. Cut each piece into quarters to serve
